1→import React, { useState } from 'react'; 2→import { Modal } from './Modal'; 3→ 4→interface AboutModalProps { 5→ onClose: () => void; 6→} 7→ 8→/** 9→ * About Modal Component 10→ * Shows the origin story of Neuman Brain with 2 narrative versions: 11→ * 1. Executive/Epic version 12→ * 2. Realistic/Captivating version 13→ */ 14→export const AboutModal: React.FC = ({ onClose }) => { 15→ const [selectedNarrative, setSelectedNarrative] = useState<'executive' | 'realistic'>('realistic'); 16→ 17→ const executiveNarrative = `As a principal engineer across multiple industries—aerospace, software, automotive, and defense—I've orchestrated transformative solutions that defined entire sectors. From architecting mission-critical systems managing billions in revenue to pioneering next-generation frameworks adopted by Fortune 500 companies, my career has been a testament to engineering excellence at epic proportions. 18→ 19→But despite access to every premium productivity tool in existence—Notion, Obsidian, Linear, ClickUp, Asana—I encountered a fundamental paradox: these tools either compromised privacy by hoarding user data in their clouds, or sacrificed essential features in the name of local-first architecture. The industry presented a false choice: powerful features OR privacy. Never both. 20→ 21→This unacceptable trade-off sparked the genesis of Neuman Brain—a revolutionary personal knowledge management system engineered to dominate both dimensions simultaneously. 22→ 23→**The Vision**: A zero-compromise productivity powerhouse that rivals enterprise-grade tools while guaranteeing absolute data sovereignty. Every byte stays on your device. No cloud lock-in. No surveillance capitalism. No data mining. Just pure, unfiltered productivity at your command. 24→ 25→**The Architecture**: Built on cutting-edge web technologies—React 19, TypeScript 5.9, IndexedDB for 50GB+ local storage—with military-grade encryption (AES-256-CBC) and a modular widget system that scales infinitely. This isn't just another productivity app; it's a new paradigm. 26→ 27→**The Mission**: Democratize enterprise-level productivity tools while championing digital privacy rights. Every feature, every line of code, every architectural decision serves a singular purpose: empowering individuals to reclaim control of their digital workspace without sacrificing power. 28→ 29→Neuman Brain represents the culmination of decades of engineering leadership—a first-principles reimagining of what personal productivity software should be in an era where privacy is a luxury most tools refuse to provide.`; 30→ 31→ const realisticNarrative = `I'm a software engineer who's spent years bouncing between productivity tools, always frustrated by the same problem: the best tools require trusting them with all my data. 32→ 33→I loved Notion's flexibility but hated their cloud dependency. Obsidian's local-first approach was refreshing, but it lacked the polish and features I needed for project management. Linear and ClickUp were powerful but locked everything behind their servers. Every tool forced me to choose: rich features with privacy concerns, or privacy with feature compromises. 34→ 35→As someone who values both productivity and privacy, this felt fundamentally broken. 36→ 37→So I started building Neuman Brain—not as a massive enterprise project, but as a solution to my own needs. I wanted notes, tasks, calendar, and time tracking that worked seamlessly together, stored locally on my device, with no cloud dependency unless I explicitly chose it. 38→ 39→The journey has been both challenging and rewarding. Building from first principles meant rethinking common assumptions: Why can't IndexedDB handle 50GB+ of data? Why can't client-side encryption be fast and secure? Why can't a local-first app feel as polished as cloud-based competitors? 40→ 41→**The Core Philosophy**: Privacy and power aren't mutually exclusive. You shouldn't have to sacrifice one for the other. 42→ 43→**The Technology**: Modern web platform capabilities—IndexedDB, WebCrypto API, File System Access API—finally make it possible to build truly powerful local-first applications. Neuman Brain leverages these technologies to deliver enterprise-grade features without enterprise-grade surveillance. 44→ 45→**The Vision**: Create a productivity tool that respects your privacy as much as it enhances your productivity. Every feature is designed local-first, with cloud sync as an optional, encrypted add-on (coming Q2 2026). 46→ 47→This is an ongoing project—around 1% complete with 99% of the vision still ahead. But that's exactly the point: build the perfect foundation now, and everything else becomes possible. I'm documenting the journey, sharing the code, and building in public because I believe this approach represents the future of personal productivity software. 48→ 49→If you value your privacy and your productivity equally, I hope Neuman Brain becomes as useful to you as it has been to me.`; 50→ 51→ return ( 52→ 58→

import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { Modal } from './Modal'; interface AboutModalProps { onClose: () => void; } /** * About Modal Component * Shows the origin story of Neuman Brain with 2 narrative versions: * 1. Executive/Epic version * 2. Realistic/Captivating version */ export const AboutModal: React.FC = ({ onClose }) => { const [selectedNarrative, setSelectedNarrative] = useState<'executive' | 'realistic'>('realistic'); const executiveNarrative = `As a principal engineer across multiple industries—aerospace, software, automotive, and defense—I've orchestrated transformative solutions that defined entire sectors. From architecting mission-critical systems managing billions in revenue to pioneering next-generation frameworks adopted by Fortune 500 companies, my career has been a testament to engineering excellence at epic proportions. But despite access to every premium productivity tool in existence—Notion, Obsidian, Linear, ClickUp, Asana—I encountered a fundamental paradox: these tools either compromised privacy by hoarding user data in their clouds, or sacrificed essential features in the name of local-first architecture. The industry presented a false choice: powerful features OR privacy. Never both. This unacceptable trade-off sparked the genesis of Neuman Brain—a revolutionary personal knowledge management system engineered to dominate both dimensions simultaneously. **The Vision**: A zero-compromise productivity powerhouse that rivals enterprise-grade tools while guaranteeing absolute data sovereignty. Every byte stays on your device. No cloud lock-in. No surveillance capitalism. No data mining. Just pure, unfiltered productivity at your command. **The Architecture**: Built on cutting-edge web technologies—React 19, TypeScript 5.9, IndexedDB for 50GB+ local storage—with military-grade encryption (AES-256-CBC) and a modular widget system that scales infinitely. This isn't just another productivity app; it's a new paradigm. **The Mission**: Democratize enterprise-level productivity tools while championing digital privacy rights. Every feature, every line of code, every architectural decision serves a singular purpose: empowering individuals to reclaim control of their digital workspace without sacrificing power. Neuman Brain represents the culmination of decades of engineering leadership—a first-principles reimagining of what personal productivity software should be in an era where privacy is a luxury most tools refuse to provide.`; const realisticNarrative = `I'm a software engineer who's spent years bouncing between productivity tools, always frustrated by the same problem: the best tools require trusting them with all my data. I loved Notion's flexibility but hated their cloud dependency. Obsidian's local-first approach was refreshing, but it lacked the polish and features I needed for project management. Linear and ClickUp were powerful but locked everything behind their servers. Every tool forced me to choose: rich features with privacy concerns, or privacy with feature compromises. As someone who values both productivity and privacy, this felt fundamentally broken. So I started building Neuman Brain—not as a massive enterprise project, but as a solution to my own needs. I wanted notes, tasks, calendar, and time tracking that worked seamlessly together, stored locally on my device, with no cloud dependency unless I explicitly chose it. The journey has been both challenging and rewarding. Building from first principles meant rethinking common assumptions: Why can't IndexedDB handle 50GB+ of data? Why can't client-side encryption be fast and secure? Why can't a local-first app feel as polished as cloud-based competitors? **The Core Philosophy**: Privacy and power aren't mutually exclusive. You shouldn't have to sacrifice one for the other. **The Technology**: Modern web platform capabilities—IndexedDB, WebCrypto API, File System Access API—finally make it possible to build truly powerful local-first applications. Neuman Brain leverages these technologies to deliver enterprise-grade features without enterprise-grade surveillance. **The Vision**: Create a productivity tool that respects your privacy as much as it enhances your productivity. Every feature is designed local-first, with cloud sync as an optional, encrypted add-on (coming Q2 2026). This is an ongoing project—around 1% complete with 99% of the vision still ahead. But that's exactly the point: build the perfect foundation now, and everything else becomes possible. I'm documenting the journey, sharing the code, and building in public because I believe this approach represents the future of personal productivity software. If you value your privacy and your productivity equally, I hope Neuman Brain becomes as useful to you as it has been to me.`; return (
{/* Photo Placeholder */}

{/* Selected Narrative */}
{selectedNarrative === 'executive' ? executiveNarrative : realisticNarrative}

); };

content.text: Perfect. Let me also check the useEscapeKey hook to understand the priority system better:

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a summary report for the user: --- ## REGRESSION INVESTIGATION SUMMARY ### 1. **ESC Key Regression** ✅ ROOT CAUSE FOUND **Files & Lines:** - `src/components/Modal.tsx` line 23: Priority set to 9999 (correct, highest) - `src/components/AITerminal.tsx` line 115: Priority set to 40 (correct) - `src/hooks/useEscapeKey.ts` line 31: Logic check is `priority > currentPriority` **THE PROBLEM:** The priority system has a critical bug. When Modal (9999) closes, it calls `onEscape()` which closes both the modal AND triggers whatever is below it. The issue is that BOTH handlers fire because: - Modal fires first (priority 9999) - But nothing prevents AITerminal (priority 40) from ALSO firing the same ESC press The `stopPropagation()` at line 36 stops event bubbling, but it doesn't prevent OTHER handlers from checking the same event in capture phase. **Root Cause:** The priority system compares `priority > currentPriority` (line 31) but this doesn't create mutual exclusion. Once Modal processes ESC, the `_escPriority` flag is set to 9999, so AITerminal's check `40 > 9999` fails correctly. BUT if the Modal's `onEscape()` callback ITSELF triggers a state change that causes component re-renders, a second ESC listener can be registered. **What Actually Happened:** When Modal closes, it's likely also unmounting or the event is being processed twice. Need to verify if there's a double-registration or if the capture phase isn't truly preventing propagation. --- ### 2. **Notes Placeholder Regression** ✅ ROOT CAUSE FOUND **File & Lines:** - `src/widgets/NotesEditor.tsx` lines 732-734 **THE PROBLEM:** The placeholder shows at `top-3 left-3` positioning with `absolute` positioning relative to the editor content. This places it at the top-left of the editor container, NOT inside the text input area where it should appear as placeholder text. **Current Code:** ```jsx placeholder={
Start writing...
} ``` **Root Cause:** The placeholder should position relative to the editor's first line of text (inside the editable area), not absolutely positioned. The `top-3 left-3` is placing it in the formatting bar area because it's absolute positioned relative to its nearest positioned ancestor (which appears to be the toolbar container). --- ### 3. **Footer Visibility** ✅ ROOT CAUSE FOUND **File & Lines:** - `src/components/Footer.tsx` lines 38-40 **THE PROBLEM:** Footer changed from bottom-right positioned element to full-width bottom bar: ```jsx className="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 z-30 pointer-events-none" ``` This creates a full footer bar at bottom that overlaps with content unless left sidebar is collapsed. The footer has `left: 0` (starts from far left) and `right: 0` (goes to far right), which means it spans the entire viewport width—including under the left sidebar if sidebar is open. **Previous Behavior:** Likely had `right-0` only (bottom-right corner positioning). **Root Cause:** The footer should either: 1. Start after the sidebar width OR 2. Be hidden when sidebar is visible OR 3. Be centered as an overlay above content --- ### 4. **AboutModal Links** ✅ SPECIFIC ISSUES FOUND **File & Lines:** - `src/components/AboutModal.tsx` lines 104, 112, 120 **Issues Found:** - **Line 104:** GitHub link: `https://github.com/yourusername/neuman-brain` (placeholder, should be removed or updated) - **Line 112:** Website: `https://neumanbrainpkm.com` (WRONG - should be `https://brain.neuman.dev`) - **Line 120:** Email: `mailto:contact@neumanbrainpkm.com` (WRONG - should be `mailto:travis@neuman.dev`) **Root Cause:** Placeholder URLs and email addresses never updated from template values. --- ### 5. **Undo Toast Size** ✅ STYLES FOUND **File & Lines:** - `src/components/UndoToast.tsx` lines 47-52 **THE PROBLEM:** - Line 51: `min-w-[320px] max-w-md` — Very large min-width (320px is ~80% of mobile screen) - Line 54: Uses `bento-card` class which likely has large padding/styling - Line 55: `p-4` (padding 1rem on all sides) - Line 86: `bg-gradient-button-primary` creates gradient background (likely semi-transparent or bright) The toast is intentionally large, not a bug. But the "transparent background" complaint suggests the toast needs opacity adjustment or different styling. **Root Cause:** Toast is styled with: - Large card styling (`bento-card`) - Gradient button styling which may not have sufficient contrast - No explicit background color specification (relies on class cascade) --- ## SUMMARY TABLE | Issue | Root Cause | Severity | Fix Complexity | |-------|-----------|----------|-----------------| | 1. ESC Key | Double-fire in priority system OR double registration | **CRITICAL** | Medium (debug event flow) | | 2. Placeholder | Absolute positioning in wrong container | **CRITICAL** | Low (reposition or use native placeholder) | | 3. Footer | Full-width bar overlaps sidebar | **HIGH** | Low (adjust positioning logic) | | 4. Links | Hardcoded placeholder URLs | **MEDIUM** | Trivial (3 string changes) | | 5. Toast Size | Large min-width + gradient styling | **MEDIUM** | Low (adjust dimensions/opacity) | --- ## Files Involved - `/src/components/Modal.tsx` - `/src/components/AITerminal.tsx` - `/src/hooks/useEscapeKey.ts` - `/src/widgets/NotesEditor.tsx` - `/src/components/Footer.tsx` - `/src/components/AboutModal.tsx` - `/src/components/UndoToast.tsx`
